---
title: "Online Appendix: Predicting College Major Field of Study"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  stevetemplates::article3:
    citation_package: biblatex
bibliography: references.bib
biblio-style: apa
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{booktabs}
  - \usepackage{longtable}
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \floatplacement{figure}{H}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.pos = "H",
  out.width = "100%"
)

library(targets)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)
library(here)

tar_load(c(
  missingness_table,
  tbl_variable_list,
  tbl_performance,
  per_class_metrics,
  tuning_results,
  recipe_check,
  cip_labels
))
```

# A. Variable List

Table A\ref{tab:variable-list} provides the complete list of predictor
variables included in the analysis, including source questionnaire, wave,
and variable type.

```{r tbl-variable-list}
tbl_variable_list %>%
  kableExtra::kbl(
    caption   = "Predictor variables: source, wave, and type.",
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    longtable = TRUE,
    col.names = c("Variable", "Label", "Source", "Wave", "Type")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "repeat_header"),
    font_size = 9
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::column_spec(2, width = "8cm")
```

# B. CIP-2 Category Collapse Decisions

[Document any CIP-2 categories collapsed due to small cell counts, including
the original categories, the collapsed category, and the rationale.]

```{r tbl-cip-collapse}
cip_labels %>%
  dplyr::select(cip2_code, cip2_label, cip2_collapsed_code, cip2_collapsed_label) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(cip2_collapsed_code, cip2_code) %>%
  kableExtra::kbl(
    caption  = "CIP-2 category definitions and collapse decisions.",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    col.names = c("Original Code", "Original Label",
                  "Collapsed Code", "Collapsed Label")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"), font_size = 9)
```

# C. Missing Data

```{r tbl-missingness}
missingness_table %>%
  dplyr::filter(pct_missing > 0) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(pct_missing = scales::percent(pct_missing, accuracy = 0.1)) %>%
  kableExtra::kbl(
    caption  = "Missingness rates for variables with any missing data.",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    col.names = c("Variable", "N Missing", "% Missing")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"))
```

Figure A\ref{fig:missingness} displays the same information graphically.

```{r fig-missingness, fig.cap="Missing data rates by predictor variable, analytic sample prior to imputation."}
knitr::include_graphics(here("outputs", "figures", "figA1_missingness.png"))
```

# D. Preprocessing Details

The recipe was applied to the training data and produced
`r recipe_check$n_predictors` predictor columns after one-hot encoding and
zero-variance filtering, from `r recipe_check$n_rows` training observations.

# E. Tuning Results

Figure A\ref{fig:tuning} shows the full distribution of cross-validated ROC
AUC across all 20 hyperparameter combinations evaluated.

```{r fig-tuning, fig.cap="Cross-validated ROC AUC across all tuning grid combinations, ranked best to worst."}
knitr::include_graphics(here("outputs", "figures", "fig2_tuning_curves.png"))
```

```{r tbl-tuning-full}
tune::collect_metrics(tuning_results) %>%
  dplyr::filter(.metric == "roc_auc") %>%
  dplyr::select(hidden_units, epochs, penalty, dropout, learn_rate, mean, std_err) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(mean)) %>%
  kableExtra::kbl(
    caption  = "Full tuning grid results: macro-weighted ROC AUC by hyperparameter combination.",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    digits   = 4,
    col.names = c("Hidden Units", "Epochs", "L2 Penalty",
                  "Dropout", "Learn Rate", "Mean AUC", "Std. Error")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"), font_size = 9)
```

# F. Per-Class Performance

```{r tbl-per-class}
per_class_metrics %>%
  dplyr::select(cip2_collapsed_label, metric, .estimate) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = metric, values_from = .estimate) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(f1)) %>%
  kableExtra::kbl(
    caption  = "Per-class precision, recall, and F1 on the held-out test set.",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    digits   = 3,
    col.names = c("Major Field", "Precision", "Recall", "F1")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"), font_size = 9)
```

# References {-}
